specs.spec: Validate must reject duplicate spec IDs, duplicate marker IDs, duplicate links, links referencing unknown specs or markers, resolution entries referencing unknown specs, unknown markers, unlinked edges, or duplicate resolution entries. Validation must pass when all references are consistent.

Given a TodoAction with a Scan covering all spec and marker IDs, the algorithm must produce a todo per linked edge where the current hash differs from the baseline and no resolution entry matches the current hashes. Edges where both sides are unchanged must produce no todo. Edges with a matching resolution entry must produce no todo.

Given a ResetAction targeting a linked edge, the algorithm must record a resolution entry with the current spec and marker hashes, then collapse all nodes whose every edge is either consistent or has a matching resolution entry. Collapse updates the baseline hash to the current scan hash and prunes resolution entries for collapsed edges. The target edge must be in links or the action errors.

Collapse runs iteratively. In each pass, any marker whose every edge is checked gets its baseline hash updated to the current scan hash and its resolution entries pruned. Same for specs. Passes repeat until no node collapses. This enables progressive collapse across many-to-many topologies.

An edge is unchecked when the current marker hash or current spec hash differs from the baseline and no resolution entry records the current hashes. If both baseline hashes match the current scan hashes, the edge is consistent and not unchecked. If a resolution entry exists with matching current hashes, the edge is not unchecked.

The scan must provide a hash for every spec and marker in the context. Missing hashes for known specs or markers cause an error. Unknown spec or marker hashes in the scan that are not in the context also cause an error.
